title: SSO Configuration for CloudDash
content:
Configuring Single Sign-On (SSO) for CloudDash enables your team to authenticate through your corporate identity provider, centralizing access control and improving security posture.

Prerequisites: Pro plan with SSO add-on ($100/month) or Enterprise plan (SSO included). Admin role in CloudDash. Admin access to your identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, OneLogin, or any SAML 2.0 compatible IdP).

Step 1 — Enable SSO in CloudDash: Settings > Security > Single Sign-On > Enable SAML SSO. CloudDash displays Service Provider (SP) metadata including ACS URL, Entity ID, and SP certificate. Download the SP metadata XML for IdP configuration.

Step 2 — Configure Identity Provider: Create a new SAML application in your IdP using CloudDash SP metadata. Set NameID format to emailAddress. Configure attribute mapping: email (required), firstName (optional), lastName (optional), groups (optional, for role mapping).

Step 3 — Upload IdP Metadata: In CloudDash SSO settings, upload your IdP metadata XML or manually enter IdP Entity ID, SSO URL, and signing certificate. CloudDash validates the certificate format on upload.

Step 4 — Configure Role Mapping: Map IdP groups to CloudDash roles. Example: IdP group 'clouddash-admins' maps to Admin, 'clouddash-editors' maps to Editor, all others default to Viewer. Role mapping is evaluated on every login.

Step 5 — Test and Enforce: Use Test SSO Login to verify the flow with your account. Once confirmed, enable 'Require SSO' to disable password-based login for all organization members. Keep at least one break-glass Admin account with password login for emergency access (Enterprise feature).

Just-In-Time Provisioning: Enable JIT to automatically create CloudDash accounts when users authenticate via SSO for the first time. Users are assigned the default role (Viewer) unless group mapping specifies otherwise. Disable JIT if you prefer pre-invite only workflows.
